draft.introduction: The Daxue ("Great Learning") outlines the Confucian program of self-cultivation in eight steps, moving from the investigation of things to the ordering of the state. It became the gateway text for all Confucian education.

translation.text: The Way of the Great Learning lies in illuminating luminous virtue, in renewing the people, and in resting in the highest good. The ancients who wished to illumine their luminous virtue throughout the world first ordered their states. Wishing to order their states, they first regulated their families. Wishing to regulate their families, they first cultivated their persons. Wishing to cultivate their persons, they first rectified their hearts. Wishing to rectify their hearts, they first made their intentions sincere. Wishing to make their intentions sincere, they first extended their knowledge. The extension of knowledge lies in the investigation of things.
translation.original_text: 大学之道，在明明德，在亲民，在止于至善

translator_notes.note: The eight steps form a chain from inner cultivation to political transformation, establishing the Confucian conviction that personal virtue and social order are inseparable. The phrase "investigation of things" (gewu) became the most debated term in Neo-Confucian philosophy: Zhu Xi interpreted it as empirical inquiry into external things, while Wang Yangming read it as the rectification of the mind's relation to things.
